mnemonics.title: Entropy Formula
mnemonics.content: E.N.T.R.O.P.Y = Negative sum of (Probability × log base 2 of Probability)

mnemonics.title: ID3 vs C4.5 vs CART
mnemonics.content: I D3 = Information Decision tree with 3 (uses Information Gain); C4.5 = C for Continuous + Gain Ratio; CART = Classification And Regression Tree (uses Gini, always binary)

mnemonics.title: Pruning Types
mnemonics.content: PRE-mature pruning stops early, POST-poned pruning cuts back later

mnemonics.title: Information Gain Purpose
mnemonics.content: IG tells us how much uncertainty REMAINING in the dataset is REDUCED by splitting

acronyms.usage: Measures reduction in entropy after splitting on an attribute: IG = H(parent) - H(children)
Normalized Information Gain used by C4.5: GR = IG / SplitInfo to reduce bias toward multi-valued attributes
Decision tree algorithm using Gini (classification) or variance (regression), always creates binary splits
First practical decision tree algorithm using Information Gain for attribute selection
Variance-based splitting criterion for regression trees: MSE = (1/n) × Σ(yᵢ - ŷ)²

visualTips.visualization: Think of entropy as a measure of 'disorder' or 'mixedness'. A coin flip with 50-50 probability is maximally disordered (entropy=1). A coin that always lands heads is perfectly ordered (entropy=0). The more mixed the classes, the higher the entropy.

Imagine a jar of colored marbles. Entropy measures how 'mixed' the colors are. Information Gain measures how much less mixed the jar becomes after separating marbles by some property (like 'shiny' vs 'dull'). More separation = higher IG.

A decision tree is literally a flowchart where each internal node asks a yes/no question, each branch represents an answer, and each leaf gives a prediction. The tree grows 'downward' from the root with each split.

Gini impurity = probability of misclassifying a randomly chosen element if we randomly assign a class according to the distribution. Higher Gini = higher chance of wrong classification = more impurity.

Pre-pruning is like stopping a tree from growing too tall too early. Post-pruning is like letting it grow fully then trimming branches. Post-pruning is like topiary - you see the full shape first then refine.

analogies.analogy: Like playing 20 questions to classify an object. Each question (node) splits possibilities into smaller groups. We choose questions that divide groups most effectively (highest Information Gain).
Entropy is like the messiness of a room. Information Gain is like how much tidier the room gets after organizing by some rule. The best rule makes the biggest improvement.

Pruning a decision tree is like pruning a real tree - cutting away unnecessary branches that may have grown due to noise in training data. Pre-pruning stops growth early; post-pruning lets it grow then trims.

Gini is like measuring impurity by counting misclassifications (simpler, faster). Entropy is like measuring impurity by calculating uncertainty (more theoretically grounded). Both aim to measure the same thing - just different formulas.
Binary splits are like asking yes/no questions (two branches). Multi-way splits are like asking multiple choice questions (many branches). CART uses only yes/no; ID3/C4.5 can use multiple choice.

Regression trees partition continuous space like classification trees partition discrete classes, but instead of predicting a class, each leaf predicts a numeric value (like the average of training samples in that region).
